title: Only the last image described in a multistage containerfile is being built
body:
### Issue Description

When building a multistage container file only the last image from the file is actually being built. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create simple multistage [ContainerFile](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12262306/ContainerFile.txt)
2. Build the images from this file with the following command: `podman build --file=$(pwd)/ContainerFile .`


### Describe the results you received

Only the last image described in the containerfile is being built this can be observed in the output of the [podman build command](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12262118/podman_build_output.txt)

Only a single untagged image is being present after the build finishes as can be observed in the [podman images output](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12262119/podman_images_output.txt)


### Describe the results you expected

I expected to see two FROM statements in the podman build uitput.
I expected to see two untagged images in my local container registry

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Podman 4.6.0 installed with brew on macOS 13.4.1

### Additional information

I noticed this first with podman 4.2.0  and then replicated it with podman 4.6.0 on my personal macOS machine.

I did not have this issue in the previous podman version I used (2.1.1)

comments.body:
I also tried the above with podman 3.4.4 in ubuntu (22.04.5 LTS) and with that version the build functioned as expected.
After updating to podman 4.6.0 the problem with the multistage container build occurs.

Hi @vanmegenNiels , Only last stage is being build because, first stage is not used in target stage, it is useless for the build.
In order to still build the first stage, either use `podman build --skip-unused-stages=false ...` or modify containerfile to actually use first stage.

Solution is 

* Either build with 

```console
podman build  --skip-unused-stages=false --file=$(pwd)/ContainerFile .
```

or  Modify container file to use first stage as well in target stage

```Dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest as Stage1
RUN echo hey > world
ENV STAGE=1

FROM alpine:latest as Stage2
COPY --from=Stage1 world .
ENV STAGE=2
```

Feel free to re-open issue if something is unresolved.
